headline: Ugandan Coffee Farmers Thrive Despite Drought
meta_description: Coffee growers in Uganda's Masaka region are producing larger, tastier yields by adopting regenerative agriculture techniques that help combat drought.
summary_short: Ugandan coffee farmers are harvesting better crops than ever by using regenerative agriculture to beat drought and unpredictable rainfall.
body:
In the rolling hills of Uganda's Masaka region, something encouraging is happening in the coffee fields. Farmers working with a pilot program have discovered that regenerative agriculture—a collection of growing techniques ranging from simple mulching to more complex cover cropping—is helping their robusta coffee plants produce larger and tastier yields.

The approach is proving especially valuable in an era of erratic rainfall and drought stress. By working with the soil rather than against it, these growers are finding that their plants become more resilient, adapting better to changing weather patterns while producing higher-quality coffee. The success in Masaka suggests that sustainable farming methods can deliver real economic benefit alongside environmental healing.
